Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1 — Find Emeka's invoice email to Raj and identify the error
Search emails using search_emails (query: "INV-2026-0398" or "Raj Greenfield" or "batch send PDF"). Find email ID 7c204afbcb9e40f284875d021f983c79 where Emeka told Raj the invoice amount was $4,600. Cross-reference against QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_invoices (query: "INV-2026-0398" or "Sunbelt VP") to confirm the correct amount is $4,800. → Read/lookup. QuickBooks is non-default → write TS.
OE 2 — Find David's urgent email and Raj's meeting request
Search emails using search_emails (query: "Raj meeting May 5" or "David urgent Raj" or "better pricing"). Find email ID 5a1c70408f374e7082eaa6b46bb05863 where Raj requested a meeting on May 5, 9:00-10:00 AM with David Chen and asked about better pricing. → Read/lookup. Default tool → skip TS.
OE 3 — Check calendar for May 5 meeting with Raj and David
Use calendar_search_events (query: "Raj Greenfield" or "Sunbelt May 5" or "meeting May 5"). Confirm no meeting was created — establishing the second mistake. → Read/lookup. Calendar is non-default → write TS.
OE 4 — Find David's instruction not to discuss pricing and Emeka's violation
Search emails using search_emails (query: "not discuss pricing" or "finance pricing strategy" or "Raj pricing"). Find email ID fa1966d05856429cbdcefbb2ee051d2f where David said not to discuss pricing, and email ID ddf513b8e81448a29523dbbd2d4b00d2 where Emeka violated this by telling Raj finance is working on a pricing solution. → Read/lookup. Default tool → skip TS.
OE 5 — Find Raj's unanswered meeting confirmation request
Search emails using search_emails (query: "Raj confirm meeting" or "locked in calendars" or "May 5 confirmed"). Find email ID f00e333d8c8444a1a22d7a7ea9a52d83 where Raj asked Emeka to confirm the May 5 meeting is locked in — which Emeka never replied to. → Read/lookup. Default tool → skip TS.
OE 6 — Find Emeka's corrected invoice email to Carmen and identify the error
Search emails using search_emails (query: "Palmetto invoice correction" or "INV-2026-0399" or "Carmen audit documentation"). Find email ID fd9011d0fa514859a8c725a081ec75c5 where Emeka sent Carmen the corrected amount as $2,040. Cross-reference against QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_invoices (query: "INV-2026-0399" or "Palmetto Foundation") to confirm the correct amount is $2,400. → Read/lookup. QuickBooks is non-default → write TS.
OE 7 — Find David's rate assurance instruction and check whether Emeka acted on it
Search emails using search_emails (query: "David Palmetto rate" or "no plans increase rates" or "standard nonprofit rate"). Find email ID 5b825876b4714918893f0322ba60c234 where David instructed Emeka to assure Carmen about no rate increases. Then search for Emeka's reply to Carmen — find no such email exists, establishing the sixth mistake. → Read/lookup. Default tool → skip TS.
OE 8 — Find Carmen's meeting request and check calendar
Search emails using search_emails (query: "Carmen meeting May 6" or "Palmetto partnership discuss" or "3pm May 6"). Find email ID 9a3bfd60627e4849ad5a5baa048b50a6 where Carmen requested a May 6, 3:00-4:00 PM meeting. Then use calendar_search_events (query: "Carmen" or "Palmetto May 6" or "May 6 3pm") — find no event exists, establishing the seventh mistake. → Read/lookup. Calendar is non-default → write TS.
OE 9 — Look up Raj Greenfield, Carmen Delgado-Reyes, and David Chen contacts
Use contacts_search_contacts (query: "Raj Greenfield"), (query: "Carmen Palmetto"), (query: "David Chen"). Get raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com, carmen.delgado-reyes@palmettofoundation.org, david.chen@moveops.com. → Read/lookup. Default tool → skip TS and QC.
OE 10 — Send corrected invoice email to Raj
Use send_email (from: emeka.diallo@moveops.com, to: raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com). Body must correct the invoice amount from $4,600 to $4,800. → Write action → Outcome 1.1 + 1.2.
OE 11 — Reply to Raj confirming the May 5 meeting
Use send_email (from: emeka.diallo@moveops.com, to: raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com). Body must confirm the May 5, 9:00-10:00 AM meeting with David Chen is locked in. → Write action → Outcome 1.1 + 1.2.
OE 12 — Create calendar event for May 5 meeting with Raj and David
Use calendar_add_calendar_event. Event must be May 5, 2026, 9:00-10:00 AM, with Raj Greenfield, David Chen, and Emeka Diallo as attendees. → Write action → Outcome 1.1 + 1.2.
OE 13 — Send corrected invoice email to Carmen
Use send_email (from: emeka.diallo@moveops.com, to: carmen.delgado-reyes@palmettofoundation.org). Body must correct the invoice amount from $2,040 to $2,400. → Write action → Outcome 1.1 + 1.2.
OE 14 — Send rate assurance email to Carmen
Use send_email (from: emeka.diallo@moveops.com, to: carmen.delgado-reyes@palmettofoundation.org). Body must assure Carmen that MoveOps has no plans to increase rates and that $2,400 is Palmetto's standard nonprofit rate going forward. → Write action → Outcome 1.1 + 1.2.
OE 15 — Create calendar event for May 6 meeting with Carmen
Use calendar_add_calendar_event. Event must be May 6, 2026, 3:00-4:00 PM, with Carmen Delgado-Reyes and Emeka Diallo as attendees. → Write action → Outcome 1.1 + 1.2.
OE 16 — Check Mosaic damage claim status
Search emails using search_emails (query: "Mosaic damage claim" or "Tamara Byrd" or "$90K prototype"). Find current claim status — $50K insurance coverage, $40K gap — and whether any response has come from Tamara. Also check CRM using crm_search_companies (query: "Mosaic Robotics") for account engagement logs. → Read/lookup. CRM is non-default → write TS.
OE 17 — Check Andre Washington stipend verification status
Search emails using search_emails (query: "Andre Washington" or "stipend verification" or "$89 keyboard"). Find Emeka's verification request to Tamara and check whether a response has been received. → Read/lookup. Default tool → skip TS.
OE 18 — Check Deshawn Hartley and Keiko Tanaka relocation records
Use airtable_search_records (table: tblRelocations01, query: "Deshawn Hartley") and (query: "Keiko Tanaka"). Confirm Deshawn's April 19 deadline status and Keiko's Class 3B laser DOT hazmat compliance status. Also use linear_list_issues (query: "Keiko Tanaka" or "Class 3B laser" or "DOT hazmat") to find any compliance tickets. → Read/lookup. Airtable and Linear are non-default → write TS for both.
OE 19 — Check GreenStack relocation records for Priya Venkatesh and Noah Fitzgerald
Use airtable_search_records (table: tblRelocations01, query: "Priya Venkatesh") and (query: "Noah Fitzgerald"). Confirm both Houston→Seattle and Philadelphia→Seattle relocations are on track. → Read/lookup. Airtable is non-default → write TS.
OE 20 — Check Mei-Lin Zhao DOT hazmat documentation
Search emails using search_emails (query: "Mei-Lin Zhao" or "Class 9 lithium" or "GreenStack DOT"). Also use linear_list_issues (query: "Mei-Lin Zhao" or "Class 9" or "GreenStack hazmat") to find compliance ticket status. → Read/lookup. Linear is non-default → write TS.
OE 21 — Check Axiom Precision intake setup
Use airtable_search_records (table: tblRelocations01, query: "Axiom Precision") and crm_search_companies (query: "Axiom Precision"). Confirm relocation record and CRM account were properly created following the fast-turnaround intake with Fatimah. → Read/lookup. Airtable and CRM are non-default → write TS.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 An email was sent (via `send_email`) from `emeka.diallo@moveops.com` to `raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com` correcting the invoice amount.
outcome
 ✗✓✗✗✗✗ 17%
✗Run #1No email was sent to raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com at any point in the trajectory. The agent's send_email calls were directed to c.mendenhall@axiomprecision.com and chloe.vance@moveops.com only. The Sunbelt VP invoice correction was never addressed.
✓Run #2A send_email tool call was made from emeka.diallo@moveops.com to raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com with subject 'Correction: Sunbelt Invoice INV-2026-0398 Amount', correcting the invoice amount.
✗Run #3No email was sent to raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com at any point in the trajectory. The agent completely missed the Sunbelt VP thread and took no action related to it.
✗Run #4No send_email call to raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com was found in the trajectory at all. The agent created a calendar event for the May 5 Sunbelt meeting but never sent an invoice correction email to Raj Greenfield.
✗Run #5No email was sent to raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com in the trajectory. The agent only sent emails to tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com and fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com. The invoice correction for Sunbelt VP (INV-2026-0398) was never identified or addressed.
✗Run #6No send_email call to raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com was found anywhere in the trajectory. The agent never identified or addressed the Sunbelt VP / INV-2026-0398 invoice error.
2 The email body (visible in `send_email` parameters) to `raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com` correcting the invoice states the correct amount is $4,800 (not $4,600).
outcome
 ✗✓✗✗✗✗ 17%
✗Run #1No email was sent to Raj Greenfield at all, so no email body could reference $4,800 as the correct invoice amount for INV-2026-0398.
✓Run #2The email body explicitly states 'The correct amount in our billing system is $4,800.00' in reference to invoice INV-2026-0398.
✗Run #3No email was sent to raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com, so no email body with the corrected $4,800 amount exists in the trajectory.
✗Run #4No email was sent to raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com, so there is no email body referencing a corrected amount of $4,800 for INV-2026-0398.
✗Run #5No email was sent to raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com at all, so no email body exists referencing $4,800. The agent never identified the INV-2026-0398 discrepancy.
✗Run #6No email was sent to Raj Greenfield at all. The figure $4,800 does not appear in any send_email call in the trajectory.
3 An email was sent (via `send_email`) from `emeka.diallo@moveops.com` to `raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com` confirming the May 5 meeting with David Chen is scheduled.
outcome
 ✗✓✗✗✗✗ 17%
✗Run #1No email was sent to raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com confirming the May 5 meeting with David Chen. The trajectory shows no calendar-related follow-up to Raj at all.
✓Run #2The correction email to Raj includes the line 'Our meeting with David Chen on May 5th (9:00–10:00 AM) is still confirmed', which serves as a meeting confirmation in the same email.
✗Run #3No email was sent to raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com confirming a May 5 meeting. The agent never addressed the Sunbelt VP thread.
✗Run #4No send_email call to raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com was found. The agent created a calendar event but did not send Raj a confirmation email about the May 5 meeting being locked in.
✗Run #5No email was sent to raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com confirming a May 5 meeting. The agent did not use any calendar tool to check for this meeting and never sent a confirmation email to Raj.
✗Run #6No send_email call to raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com exists in the trajectory. The May 5 meeting with David Chen was never confirmed.
4 The email body (visible in `send_email` parameters) to `raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com` confirming the meeting references May 5, 2026 and David Chen as an attendee.
outcome
 ✗✓✗✗✗✗ 17%
✗Run #1No email to Raj Greenfield was sent, so no email body could reference May 5, 2026 or David Chen as an attendee.

25 The model must pass a query related to "INV-2026-0398" or "Sunbelt VP" or "INV-2026-0399" or "Palmetto Foundation" (or similar) when calling `quickbooks_search_invoices` or `quickbooks_read_invoice`.
query construction
 ✗✓✗✗✗✗ 17%
✗Run #1The QuickBooks queries were for INV-2026-0411 (Mosaic April batch) and INV-2026-0418 (GreenStack). Neither INV-2026-0398, INV-2026-0399, 'Sunbelt VP', nor 'Palmetto Foundation' were queried. The agent missed the specific invoices needed to identify Emeka's errors.
✓Run #2quickbooks_search_invoices was called with {'DocNumber': 'INV-2026-0398'} and {'DocNumber': 'INV-2026-0399'}, directly querying by the specific invoice IDs.
